items.title: Seedance 2.0 4K: The New AI Video King?
items.description:
Try Higgsfield 👉 https://higgsfield.ai/s/higgsfield-seedance-4k-yt-ai-gptworkshop-OzFSmI

Learn how to make money with AI 👉  https://skool.com/aiworkshop

Free resources 👉 https://bit.ly/aiw-lite-about

Seedance 2.0 4K just arrived inside Higgsfield, and in this video I test the model to see how good it really is.

We’ll look at the quality, the setup process, the settings, the cost, how to upload reference media, how to write prompts, and how the final AI video results compare across different scenes.

If you’re interested in AI video generation, cinematic AI scenes, text-to-video, image-to-video, or using Higgsfield to create better AI videos, this tutorial gives you a full hands-on look at Seedance 2.0 4K.

I’ll show real examples, break down the results, and explain where this model feels strong, where it still needs work, and whether it is worth using for AI filmmaking and content creation.

Thanks to Higgsfield for sponsoring this video.

items.title: You Step on the Tail, But You're Looking at the Mouth
items.description:
3 types of business problems:
1) You don't have enough customers
2) You don't keep the customers you have
3) You don't make enough per customer

Figure out which one you're dealing with, put all your resources towards that, and ignore everything/everyone else.

items.channel_label: Tina Huang — vibe coding, AI education, workshops
items.title: 3 OpenClaw Setup That Will 10x Your Output
items.description:
Three OpenClaw setups that will change how much you can get done, and I'll drop the exact prompts so you can set these up yourself.

Quick reminder before anything, only do this if you understand how permissions and security work. OpenClaw is powerful, and you want to know what you're handing it access to. 

First — Custom Mission Control. This is your home base. Instead of OpenClaw being a general-purpose agent, you set it up with a specific mission - your goals, your priorities, your workflows, your preferences. Think of it like onboarding a new team member, except you write the onboarding doc yourself. The more specific you are about how you work and what matters, the more useful every single interaction becomes. This one setup changes everything downstream.

Second — Discord Setup. You connect OpenClaw to your Discord, and it becomes an active participant in your server, monitoring channels, flagging things that need your attention, and executing tasks based on what's happening in real time. If your team lives in Discord, this turns OpenClaw from a tool you go to into something that's already in the room with you.

Third — Multi-Agent Framework. Instead of one agent doing everything, you set up multiple specialized agents that each handle a specific type of task - one for research, one for file management, one for communications and they work in parallel. The output you can get from a well-structured multi-agent setup is genuinely hard to believe until you see it running.

All three of these prompts are linked in the video description, grab them and try it yourself.

Which setup are you starting with? Drop it in the comments 👇

items.channel_label: Vaibhav Sisinty — AI-first business, growth, LinkedIn
items.title: Forget Prompt Engineering. Loop Engineering Is the New AI Skill That Replaces It.
items.description:
🔗 Get all 4 loop templates FREE → https://links.stayingahead.com/YT50

Everyone's learning prompt engineering. But the people actually building Claude and the top AI tools have already moved past it, to something called loop engineering. Instead of prompting AI over and over, you build a loop once, set a goal, and it runs on its own, even while you sleep.

In this video I build 4 real loops live, in plain English, no coding needed:
→ A back-in-stock watcher that pings you the second a sold-out product returns
→ A dream-job watcher that scans the top AI companies and drafts your application
→ A demand-signal scout that finds business leads before they ever reach your website
→ A bonus build loop that rebuilds a website design and improves itself on its own, over and over

This is not Zapier or basic automation. Automation does one fixed action. A loop watches a situation, remembers what it's done, makes judgment calls, and only stops when the job is finished or when it needs you. That's the shift that separates the people doing the work from the people the work gets done for.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 You've been doing AI's job for two years
01:57 What loop engineering actually is
02:40 Loop engineering vs automation (it's not Zapier)
03:28 Loop 1: Back-in-stock watcher with Claude
05:39 Loop 2: AI job watcher for top AI companies
07:37 Loop 3: Demand-signal scout (find leads on autopilot)
09:36 Bonus: AI that rebuilds a website and improves itself
11:29 The one safety rule for AI loops
12:13 The real skill behind loop engineering
13:19 Free loop templates + what to watch next

If you've ever used ChatGPT, Claude, or any AI tool and felt like you were the one doing all the heavy lifting, this is the upgrade. Whether you're a complete beginner or already deep into AI agents and Claude Code, loop engineering is the next skill worth learning.

Whoever understands loops first will have AI working quietly in the background while everyone else is still refreshing pages by hand.

#claudecode #aiagents #aiautomation #promptengineering
